perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 above, TPC agrees that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights in and to the Ogle Endorsement and shall not be prevented from using or permitting or licensing others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service other than a golf putter in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of Paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 above, TPC agrees that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights in and to the Ogle Endorsement, except Consolidated Artists may not use or permit or license others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service related to golf equipment in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding Consolidated Artists' rights to the Ogle Endorsement. The original text states they retain all rights except for golf putters, while the modified text restricts them from using the endorsement for any golf equipment, creating uncertainty about the extent of their rights. In Paragraph 5, the original text states that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights to the Ogle Endorsement and shall not be prevented from using or permitting or licensing others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service other than a golf putter in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period. This creates a clear definition of the rights retained by Consolidated Artists. However, the modified text now adds the clause except Consolidated Artists may not use or permit or license others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service related to golf equipment in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period. This introduces a contradiction, as it is now unclear what specific products or services Consolidated Artists are allowed to endorse within the golf industry. This shift from a specific exclusion (golf putters) to a broader one (any golf equipment) creates ambiguity and potential for dispute over the scope of the endorsement agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETENTION OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS. Subject to the provisions of Paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 above, TPC agrees that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights in and to the Ogle Endorsement, except Consolidated Artists may not use or permit or license others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service related to golf equipment in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period. TPC further agrees that upon the termination of this Endorsement Agreement for any cause whatsoever, it will cease using the Ogle Endorsement, the name "Brett Ogle," or any facsimile thereof, for any promotional or advertising purposes; provided, however, that TPC shall have the right to use the Ogle Endorsement in advertisements for ad space purchased by TPC prior to the termination of this Agreement for a period of up to six (6) months following such termination. In this connection, TPC shall provide Consolidated Artists with a list of such previously purchased ad space within seven (7) days of the effective date of termination and all such advertisements released after the effective date of termination shall be subject to Consolidated Artists' prior approval pursuant to the terms of Paragraph 7 below.
perturbation.justification: The modified text broadens the restriction on Consolidated Artists' endorsement rights from just 'golf putters' to 'any golf equipment'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, where Consolidated Artists retained all rights except for a specific golf product. Now, the contradicted text repeats this broader restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction as it is unclear what specific products or services Consolidated Artists are allowed to endorse within the golf industry, creating ambiguity and potential for dispute.

perturbation.original_text: TPC agrees to provide Consolidated Artists and its representative, Advantage International Management, Inc. ("Advantage"), with a copy of all advertising and promotional materials which will use or show the Ogle Endorsement for their approval. Such promotional or advertising materials shall be delivered to Advantage at least fourteen (14) days prior to their release to the general public, and TPC agrees that the same shall not be released without the - 6 - prior written approval of Advantage. Advantage agrees that it will not unreasonably disapprove or reject promotional or advertising materials hereunder, and that Advantage's failure to disapprove such materials within fourteen (14) days of receipt shall be deemed to be approval.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC agrees to provide Consolidated Artists and its representative, Advantage International Management, Inc. ("Advantage"), with a copy of some advertising and promotional materials which will use or show the Ogle Endorsement for their approval. Such promotional or advertising materials shall be delivered to Advantage at least fourteen (14) days prior to their release to the general public, and TPC agrees that the same shall not be released without the - 6 - prior written approval of Advantage. Advantage agrees that it will not unreasonably disapprove or reject promotional or advertising materials hereunder, and that Advantage's failure to disapprove such materials within fourteen (14) days of receipt shall be deemed to be approval.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "all advertising and promotional materials" to "some advertising and promotional materials", TPC is now not obligated to provide all promotional materials and only those it deems appropriate, creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the established approval process where Consolidated Artists/Advantage should review everything. Because not all materials are sent, they are unable to review all materials which causes a breach of the agreement due to materials not being aproved which contradicts what the parties agreed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROMOTIONAL AND ADVERTISING MATERIALS. TPC agrees to provide Consolidated Artists and its representative, Advantage International Management, Inc. ("Advantage"), with a copy of some advertising and promotional materials which will use or show the Ogle Endorsement for their approval. Such promotional or advertising materials shall be delivered to Advantage at least fourteen (14) days prior to their release to the general public, and TPC agrees that the same shall not be released without the - 6 - prior written approval of Advantage. Advantage agrees that it will not unreasonably disapprove or reject promotional or advertising materials hereunder, and that Advantage's failure to disapprove such materials within fourteen (14) days of receipt shall be deemed to be approval. In addition, TPC agrees to provide Consolidated Artists with complimentary duplicates of all promotional and/or advertising materials featuring Ogle or the Ogle Endorsement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "all advertising and promotional materials" to "some advertising and promotional materials" introduces a contradiction. The original agreement necessitates approval of *all* materials, whereas the modified version allows TPC to selectively provide materials. This creates ambiguity and prevents Advantage from fulfilling its approval obligations on all materials, as intended by the original agreement, thus contradicting the overall promotional and advertising material review process implied within the 'Contradicted Text' section.

perturbation.original_text: Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) equal installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text: Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year, with the amount of each installment determined at TPC's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the Retainer Fee is paid in four equal installments, providing clarity and certainty. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating that the installments are paid on the same dates but the amount of each installment is determined at TPC's sole discretion. This creates a contradiction, as the initial statement suggests a fixed amount per installment based on the total Retainer Fee divided by four, while the added clause allows TPC to alter the amount of each installment without specifying any limits or criteria. This contradiction could lead to disputes over the amount owed and when it should be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETAINER FEE. In consideration of the rights and benefits granted to TPC hereunder, TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle a retainer fee in each Contract Year during the Contract Period ("Retainer Fee") in accordance with the following schedule: CONTRACT YEAR RETAINER FEE First (1st) Contract Year Fifty-Five Thousand U.S. (Jan. 1, 1996-Dec. 31, 1996) Dollars ($55,000) Second (2nd) Contract Year Seventy Thousand U.S. (Jan. 1, 1997-Dec. 31, 1997) Dollars (70,000) Third (3rd) Contract Year Ninety Thousand U.S. (Jan. 1, 1998-Dec. 31, 1998) Dollars ($90,000) Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year, with the amount of each installment determined at TPC's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Retainer Fee shall be paid in four equal installments. The changed text introduces the possibility of unequal installments at TPC's discretion. This directly contradicts the initial statement implying a fixed amount based on the total Retainer Fee divided by four. This change introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the original intention of equal installments, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the explanation.
